name: Burkina Faso

colors.symbolism: the revolution that brought the present government to power
hope and abundance; the country's agricultural and natural wealth

description: Two equal horizontal stripes — red over green — with a single yellow five-pointed star centred on the dividing line.
history: Adopted on 4 August 1984 when President Thomas Sankara renamed the country from Upper Volta (Haute-Volta) to Burkina Faso ('land of upright people'). The previous Upper Volta flag had been a black-white-red horizontal triband recalling the three Volta rivers; the new design replaced it with the Pan-African red-green-yellow palette to symbolise the break with the colonial past and solidarity with other African nations.

features.symbolism: red for the revolution, green for the country's natural wealth and hope; the colours together with the central yellow star are the Pan-African colours
the guiding light of the revolution; the country's mineral wealth
